title: Chores at Home & Describing Family Members

dialogue.bot: Hello! Thank you for joining this short interview. We're going to talk a bit about your family life. First, can you tell me what chores you usually do?
dialogue.expected: I usually wash the dishes and tidy my bedroom.

dialogue.bot: That's great. How often do you wash the dishes?
dialogue.expected: I wash the dishes six times a week, on every day except Sunday.

dialogue.bot: What about tidying your bedroom? How often do you do that?
dialogue.expected: I tidy my bedroom twice a week, on Wednesdays and Saturdays.

dialogue.bot: Now, what about your sister or brother? What chores does he or she do?
dialogue.expected: My sister usually dusts the furniture.

dialogue.bot: How often does she dust the furniture?
dialogue.expected: She dusts the furniture twice a week, on Tuesdays and Fridays.

dialogue.bot: Let's move on to talk about the personality of your family members. Can you tell me about your brother or another family member?
dialogue.expected: I think my brother is unreliable.
Because he never arrives on time.

dialogue.bot: How about your sister? Can you tell me one thing about her?
dialogue.expected: My sister is easygoing, so she shares everything with me.

dialogue.bot: That sounds lovely. Finally, would you like to tell me about another family member? Perhaps your mother or father?
dialogue.expected: My father is intelligent, so he passes all tests with high scores.

dialogue.bot: Thank you very much for sharing these interesting details about your family life!

extra_questions: What other chores do you do at home?
Who helps you the most in your family?
